2025-10-01.title: Can This City Make Residents Put Down Their Smartphones?

2025-10-24.abstract: The governing party placed a distant third in a special election for a district of Wales it has dominated for a century. Plaid Cymru, a center-left Welsh nationalist party, won.

2025-10-25.title: ‘You Love It or You Hate It’: Pumpkin Spice Lattes Divide Europe
2025-10-25.abstract: One of America’s favorite fall drinks shows up in Brussels and Antwerp, but rarely in Paris and Rome. It has yet to reach the ubiquity — or the sugar content — of its stateside counterpart.

2025-10-25.title: Presidents Are Limited to Two Terms. He’s Running for His Fourth.
2025-10-25.abstract: The main opposition candidates in the Ivory Coast’s presidential election have been barred from the race. Critics say the vote has been rigged.

2025-10-25.title: Asia, Caught in the Middle, Faces Trump and Xi’s Tug of War
2025-10-25.abstract: President Trump’s visit shows how the United States and China are vying for influence in Asia over trade, technology and the fate of Taiwan.

2025-10-25.title: Where Trump Is Headed in Asia, and What Each Country Wants From Him
2025-10-25.abstract: From Kuala Lumpur to Gyeongju, President Trump is casting himself as a deal-maker and peace negotiator, while a wary region looks for tariff relief and steadier ties.

2025-10-25.title: Vegan Menu for Hosting Prince Leaves a Chef From the Amazon Fuming
2025-10-25.abstract: He was asked to cater a climate event for Prince William and 700 guests. But omitting the famous Amazonian fish was like “asking Iron Maiden to play jazz.”

2025-10-26.title: With Trump’s Asia Trip, Speculation Mounts of a Meeting With Kim Jong-un
2025-10-26.abstract: President Trump has repeatedly said that he wants to see the North Korean leader again. Mr. Kim has indicated that he is open to the idea — but only under a strict condition.

2025-10-26.title: A Break-In That Shook France
2025-10-26.abstract: The robbery at the Louvre left behind more than broken glass. It battered the pride of a nation that is increasingly glum about itself and its direction.

2025-10-26.title: 3 Years Ago It Was a Casting Agency. Now It Has $1 Billion in Drone Contracts.
2025-10-26.abstract: Among the flood of Ukrainian defense start-ups, one stands out to both its supporters and its critics: the drone maker Fire Point.

2025-10-26.title: Argentina’s Voters Hand Javier Milei a Crucial Victory in Midterm Election
2025-10-26.abstract: The result, which gives Mr. Milei enough legislative support to keep his vetoes from being overturned, showed that many voters still back the president’s libertarian experiment.

2025-10-26.title: How Venezuela’s Autocrat Uses Crypto to Fight Trump’s Sanctions
2025-10-26.abstract: President Nicolás Maduro’s opponents hope a new period of economic pain will finally topple his government. He is using cryptocurrency to hold on.

2025-10-27.title: Britain Needs China’s Money. It Fears What Comes With It.
2025-10-27.abstract: A botched spying case prompted accusations that the British government was soft on China, highlighting tensions between economic and national security interests.

2025-10-27.title: 2 U.S. Navy Aircraft Go Down in South China Sea
2025-10-27.abstract: A helicopter and a fighter jet went down in separate incidents while operating from a U.S. aircraft carrier on Sunday, the Navy said. The crews were rescued.

2025-10-27.title: U.S. Assessment of Israeli Shooting of Journalist Divided American Officials
2025-10-27.abstract: A U.S. colonel has gone public with his concern that official findings about the 2022 killing of a Palestinian American reporter were soft-pedaled to appease Israel.

2025-10-27.title: The Anatomy of a Canadian City’s Fight Over Fluoride
2025-10-27.abstract: A contentious vote over the return of fluoride to Calgary’s water was supposed to settle a decades-long debate. But the battle on both sides remains as polarized as ever.

2025-10-27.title: Is the U.S. Losing in Vietnam? Russia, North Korea and China Are Gaining.
2025-10-27.abstract: U.S. allies worry that American volatility and Russian outreach and arms sales, in particular, are driving Vietnam into a new phase.

2025-10-28.title: Ivory Coast President Secures 4th Term in Election Without Top Opponents
2025-10-28.abstract: President Alassane Ouattara won 89.77 percent of the vote, but critics called the elections flawed after two main challengers were barred from running.

2025-10-28.title: Beyond Trade War, China’s Xi Looks to Press Trump on Taiwan
2025-10-28.abstract: China’s leader wants to weaken American support for Taiwan. But first he will want clarity about President Trump’s stance toward the island.

2025-10-28.title: Jamaica’s Beach Towns and Fishing Villages Brace for Hurricane Melissa
2025-10-28.abstract: Stranded vacationers on the island, which depends on tourism, used their beds to block their windows in case the powerful storm shattered the glass.

2025-10-28.title: Trump Bonds With Japan’s Leader Over Baseball and U.S. Beef
2025-10-28.abstract: The president and Sanae Takaichi, the Japanese prime minister, traded compliments in Tokyo, but signaled no major breakthrough in resolving ongoing trade issues.

2025-10-28.title: Man Pleads Guilty to Killing Shinzo Abe, Former Prime Minister of Japan
2025-10-28.abstract: Tetsuya Yamagami, 45, is accused of assassinating Mr. Abe with a homemade gun during an election rally in 2022.

2025-10-29.title: Annabel Goldsmith, a Queen of British High Society, Dies at 91
2025-10-29.abstract: Annabel’s, the club that Ms. Goldsmith’s first husband named after her, opened in the 1960s and is still one of London’s most exclusive nightspots.

2025-10-29.title: As the Dutch Vote, One Issue Carries the Day: Affordable Housing
2025-10-29.abstract: The small European nation of 18 million people is in the middle of its worst housing crunch in decades. But there is no quick political solution.

2025-10-29.title: How the 19th-Century Opium War Shapes Xi’s Trade Clash With Trump
2025-10-29.abstract: China’s leader draws on lessons from Lin Zexu, a Qing official whose defiance of Britain led to China’s humiliating defeat but made him a national hero.

2025-10-29.title: The Netherlands’ Election Is a Referendum on the Far Right
2025-10-29.abstract: Geert Wilders and his far-right party blew up the Dutch governing coalition this year. Will that cost their party seats in Parliament?

2025-10-29.title: Hurricane Melissa’s Direct Hit Knocks Most of Jamaica Offline
2025-10-29.abstract: Most of the island of two million people was without internet service, and major airports were closed. Kingston, the capital, was mostly spared.

2025-10-30.title: Melissa Leaves Black River, Centuries-Old Jamaican Port Town, ‘Totally Destroyed’
2025-10-30.abstract: At least two people in the town died, and its historical buildings were reduced to rubble.

2025-10-30.title: Jamaica and Haiti Tally Hurricane Victims as Melissa Slams Cuba
2025-10-30.abstract: About 20 people, including children, died in a Haitian community and at least three bodies were found in Jamaica, officials said, as they began to assess the huge storm’s damage.

2025-10-30.title: Inside the Heist That Shocked the World
2025-10-30.abstract: More than a week after thieves made off with treasures from the Louvre, a picture is emerging of a seemingly well-planned burglary that exploited security lapses at the museum and outpaced the police.

2025-10-30.title: Hundreds of Thousands Have Fled Their Homes in Syria’s Rocky Transition
2025-10-30.abstract: More than 400,000 Syrians have been displaced in the year since the civil war ended, according to the United Nations, driven by a mix of sectarian violence, acts of revenge and property disputes.

2025-10-30.title: Trump’s Test Threat Could Fuel Nuclear Tensions With China
2025-10-30.abstract: Beijing has been expanding its arsenal, and distrust between China and the United States over nuclear weapons has deepened, with little hope of an agreement.

2025-10-31.title: Jamaica Prepared a Financial Fortress for Disaster. Hurricane Melissa Will Test It.
2025-10-31.abstract: All of Jamaica’s financial defenses — insurance, bonds and credit lines — could be deployed to recover from Hurricane Melissa. Will it be enough?

2025-10-31.title: Canada’s Other Superpower Problem
2025-10-31.abstract: The leaders of Canada and China met for the first time in eight years to try to reset relations after years of acrimony.

2025-10-31.title: Ukraine Gamifies the War: 40 Points to Destroy a Tank, 12 to Kill a Soldier
2025-10-31.abstract: Drone teams compete to ascend a scoreboard that rewards units for successful attacks. Ukrainian officials say the contest helps keep soldiers motivated.

2025-10-31.title: The Mystery Lady in the Governor’s Palace? Maybe a Ghost, He Says
2025-10-31.abstract: When a figure appeared in the window of a government building, the governor suggested she was an empress’s ghost. Months later, people are still talking about it.

2025-10-31.title: How Saudi Arabia and Qatar Benefited From Rule Changes to Reach the World Cup
2025-10-31.abstract: A soccer governing body that both countries have helped bankroll gave them extra rest and home-field advantage to set up World Cup qualification.
